# ai-quarantine

> Seven-stage AI artifact admission-control pipeline

## What this project does

ai-quarantine is a Python library and service that verifies, scans, and
smoke-tests AI model artifacts (GGUF, safetensors, diffusion directories)
before promoting them to a trusted registry.

## Architecture

- quarantine/pipeline.py — core 7-stage scanning pipeline (1,900 lines)
- quarantine/watcher.py — filesystem watcher that runs pipeline on new artifacts
- quarantine/audit_chain.py — hash-chained append-only audit log

## Pipeline stages

1. Source policy gate: verify origin URL against allowlist
2. Format gate: validate magic bytes, reject pickle/pt/bin
3. Integrity gate: SHA-256 hash pinning
4. Provenance gate: cosign signature verification
5. Static scan: modelscan + fickling + entropy + weight stats + gguf-guard
6. Behavioral smoke test: 41 adversarial prompts + garak (GGUF only)
7. Diffusion deep scan: config integrity validation

## Key types

- run_pipeline(path, hash, policy) -> {"passed": bool, "reason": str, "details": dict}
- run_pipeline_directory(path, hash, policy) -> same
- AuditChain(log_path) — hash-chained audit logger

## Integration

- Uses modelscan, fickling, garak, gguf-guard as optional external scanners
- Promotes artifacts to ai-model-registry via HTTP API
- Watcher mode polls a directory for new artifacts
- Container deployment via Containerfile
